Now, it comes to another very important sutana concerning karma, 3.10.99, the Buddha said, monks, if anyone should say, even as a man commits a deep karma, so does he correspondingly experience its result.
This being so, there is no living of the holy life, there is no opportunity manifested for the utter destruction of ill.
But if one should say, even as a man commits a deep, that is to be felt, so does he correspondingly experience its result.
This being so monks, there is living of the holy life, there is opportunity manifested for the utter ending of ill.
Now, for instance, there may be some trifling, evil deed of some person, which may take him to hell.
Again, there may be a trifling, evil deed of some other person, which is to be experienced in this very life.
Not much of it, name, not a dot of it is seen here after, I just talk for a while to comment.
Here, the Buddha is saying that when we commit karma, when we do a deep and action intentionally, we don't get the corresponding result always.
It all depends on the person, to all depends on the person. I will suppose that you do an evil deed, that you kill a person.
And for one person, perhaps by killing, by doing killing, in the next life, or the next few lives, he may experience being killed either once or many times.
But another person, he does the killing, but he does not experience being killed in the next life.
So this is karma, it's not very straightforward, it depends very much on the person, the person doing the deep.
This important principle that we have to understand, because the Buddha says sometimes a person does a small evil deed, and it can bring him to hell.
Another person does the same small evil deed, he may experience it in this very life.
Maybe he feels remorse, then he has done it, and then he troubles him a bit, and then next life is all clear, it doesn't have to be anymore.

even a dot of it is seen here after.

But you take the same size of salt, same lump of salt, you put in the river and you stir it and you drink the water is not salty.
So what's the difference here? The difference is that the river contains a lot of water and the water dilutes the effect of the salt.
So the water represents the good karma, salt represents evil karma.
So when we do a lot of good karma, it dilutes the effect of the evil karma that is why here, that the only way we can dilute the effect of our evil past karma is to do a lot of good now.
